original_abstract:
Analytical and functional comparability is the cornerstone of biosimilar development. In this study, we conducted a comprehensive characterization of GP2013, a proposed biosimilar to rituximab, using an extensive array of state-of-the-art analytical tools. Our physicochemical assessment demonstrated that GP2013 is highly similar to the originator rituximab across all structural levels. Mass spectrometry and peptide mapping confirmed an identical primary amino acid sequence and disulfide bridging pattern. Furthermore, a suite of orthogonal techniques—including circular dichroism, FTIR, HDX-MS, NMR, and X-ray crystallography—revealed that the higher-order three-dimensional structure of GP2013 is indistinguishable from the reference product.

While minor differences were observed in low-abundance glycan species and a trace C-terminal amidated proline variant was identified in GP2013, these were thoroughly evaluated and found to have no impact on biological function or safety. In-depth functional characterization using surface plasmon resonance (SPR) for Fc-receptor binding and a series of cell-based bioassays (binding, ADCC, CDC, and apoptosis) confirmed that GP2013 possesses the same biological properties and potency as the originator. These results collectively establish a high degree of structural and functional similarity, providing a robust foundation for the clinical development of GP2013.
humanized_abstract:
We herein addressed the analytical and functional comparability of a proposed rituximab biosimilar, namely GP2013. The physicochemical characterization of GP2013 by a battery of highly resolved analytical methods was presented. The results showed that GP2013 has the same physicochemical properties as the innovator rituximab at all levels. The same sequence, same disulfide bridges were confirmed using MS and peptide mapping analyses. In addition, a combination of CD, FTIR, HDX-MS, NMR and X-ray crystallography data confirmed that the tertiary structure of the proposed rituximab biosimilar is identical to that of the innovator rituximab.

Compared to the originator, GP2013 exhibited minor changes in low-abundance glycan structures and a trace amount of C-terminal amidated proline at the C-terminus of the heavy chain. After careful analysis, neither of these variations is expected to affect the biological activity or safety of the product. To confirm the function of GP2013, a battery of cell-based bioassays (binding, ADCC, CDC and apoptosis) were performed using SPR technology to confirm the Fc-receptor binding ability, and the bioassays confirmed that GP2013 has similar biological activity and efficacy as compared to the originator.
